headline: Alphabet’s Pentagon AI Shift Puts Ethics Risks And Valuation In Focus
summary: Alphabet (NasdaqGS:GOOGL) has revised its U.S. Department of Defense agreement to permit Google AI models in classified military applications. The expanded Pentagon work has triggered fresh employee protests and renewed calls for tighter AI ethics safeguards inside the company. Alphabet has exited a Pentagon AI drone project, citing resource constraints and prompting questions about internal views on military contracts. Investor activists are pressing new climate and AI ethics proposals,...

content.title: Alphabet’s Pentagon AI Shift Puts Ethics Risks And Valuation In Focus

The expanded Pentagon work has triggered fresh employee protests and renewed calls for tighter AI ethics safeguards inside the company. Alphabet has exited a Pentagon AI drone project, citing resource constraints and prompting questions about internal views on military contracts. headline: Samsung Electronics sees robust AI demand after Q1 profit surges eightfold to set record
summary: Samsung Electronics on Thursday reported record quarterly profit after an eightfold jump in earnings, saying it expects the momentum to continue as clients ‌spend aggressively on AI infrastructure and drive up prices of its memory chips.  A boom ‌in the construction of AI data centres has spurred chipmakers to allocate more production capacity to advanced chips that Nvidia ​uses in its so-called AI accelerators, squeezing the supply of more conventional chips.  Samsung said it "expects server memory demand to remain strong as hyperscalers accommodate enterprises' increasing adoption of AI" and large language model services.

content.title: Samsung Electronics sees robust AI demand after Q1 profit surges eightfold to set record
content.summary: Samsung Electronics on Thursday reported record quarterly profit after an eightfold jump in earnings, saying it expects the momentum to continue as clients ‌spend aggressively on AI infrastructure and drive up prices of its memory chips.  A boom ‌in the construction of AI data centres has spurred chipmakers to allocate more production capacity to advanced chips that Nvidia ​uses in its so-called AI accelerators, squeezing the supply of more conventional chips.  Samsung said it "expects server memory demand to remain strong as hyperscalers accommodate enterprises' increasing adoption of AI" and large language model services.

thumbnail.caption: FILE PHOTO: Samsung flags eightfold jump in Q1 profit as AI chip demand drives up prices

headline: Capgemini's first-quarter revenue up 7%, in line with estimates
summary: The company, which serves government agencies, critical infrastructure operators and ​large regulated businesses, said growth would have been 11% without currency swings. CEO Aiman Ezzat told reporters AI is rapidly reshaping the company's business mix, with generative and agentic ‌AI accounting for ⁠more than 10% of group bookings in the quarter. He cited Capgemini's global reach and deeper ⁠partnerships with U.S. tech firms, including Google and a new "Frontier Alliance" with OpenAI, as key drivers of growth.

content.title: Capgemini's first-quarter revenue up 7%, in line with estimates
content.summary: The company, which serves government agencies, critical infrastructure operators and ​large regulated businesses, said growth would have been 11% without currency swings. CEO Aiman Ezzat told reporters AI is rapidly reshaping the company's business mix, with generative and agentic ‌AI accounting for ⁠more than 10% of group bookings in the quarter. He cited Capgemini's global reach and deeper ⁠partnerships with U.S. tech firms, including Google and a new "Frontier Alliance" with OpenAI, as key drivers of growth.

headline: DTE Energy reports first quarter 2026 accomplishments, investments and financial results
summary: DTE Energy (NYSE: DTE) reported today that it has invested more than $1.2 billion in its utilities during the first quarter of 2026 and remains on pace to invest over $6 billion this year. These strategic investments are strengthening electric reliability, expanding clean energy resources, and ensuring the safety and dependability of DTE's natural gas services, all while keeping energy costs as affordable as possible for customers.

headline: OG&E Announces Landmark Contract with Google
summary: Today, OG&E, the operating subsidiary of locally-headquartered OGE Energy Corp. (NYSE: OGE), announced that it will power three new data centers that Google announced in Muskogee and Stillwater last year. The data centers and associated Electric Service Agreements will provide economic growth for local communities and the state, contribute to grid stability, and benefit OG&E's current customers.

headline: Taiwan's Delta Electronics warns costs rising as AI data centre demand surges
summary: Delta Electronics, a major provider of power supply and ‌cooling solutions for AI datacentres, warned that it ‌expects costs to rise in the coming quarters, with oil prices ​rising and ongoing material shortages.  • The company said its capacity remained tight and ‌it was expanding in ⁠China, Thailand, the United States and Taiwan.  • ​Delta Electronics' ​customers include Nvidia and ​major cloud service providers ‌such as Google and Meta Platforms.

headline: Stock Futures Fall as Investor Weigh Up Big Tech Earnings, Iran Fears
summary: Stocks looked set to edge higher on Thursday as investors tried to make sense of a deluge of Big Tech earnings and a sudden jump in oil prices.  S&P 500 futures climbed 0.1% and contracts tied to the tech-heavy Nasdaq 100 added 0.2%.  The three major indexes struggled for direction on Wednesday after the Federal Reserve kept interest rates unchanged, as was widely expected.

content.title: Stock Futures Fall as Investor Weigh Up Big Tech Earnings, Iran Fears
content.summary: Stocks looked set to edge higher on Thursday as investors tried to make sense of a deluge of Big Tech earnings and a sudden jump in oil prices.  S&P 500 futures climbed 0.1% and contracts tied to the tech-heavy Nasdaq 100 added 0.2%.  The three major indexes struggled for direction on Wednesday after the Federal Reserve kept interest rates unchanged, as was widely expected.

headline: Google Cloud pulls ahead as Big Tech's AI bet swells to $700 billion
summary: Alphabet's blowout cloud growth has reset expectations across major tech companies, leaving investors to recalibrate which firms are delivering the clearest returns.  Alphabet shares jumped more than 6% in premarket trading on Thursday, while Meta stock fell ‌nearly 9%.  Amazon shares rose 2.6%, while those of Microsoft  dipped 1.8%.

headline: What Apple's new CEO announcement signals ahead of earnings?
summary: Wall Street investors have been abuzz around Apple (AAPL) this past week after CEO Tim Cook announced his plans to depart from the chief executive role this September, handing the reins off to John Ternus. Apple is also expected to report its latest earnings results this Thursday, April 30. Baird senior research analyst of cloud software William Power comes on Market Domination to discuss these headlines making waves ahead of Apple's earnings release.

content.title: What Apple's new CEO announcement signals ahead of earnings?
content.description:
Wall Street investors have been abuzz around Apple (AAPL) this past week after CEO Tim Cook announced his plans to depart from the chief executive role this September, handing the reins off to John Ternus. Apple is also expected to report its latest earnings results this Thursday, April 30.

Baird senior research analyst of cloud software William Power comes on Market Domination to discuss these headlines making waves ahead of Apple's earnings release.

content.title: Alphabet Pops 6% on Q1 Earnings: Is the Full-Stack AI Strategy Finally Paying Off?

headline: Fed Inflation Gauge Hits 2-Year High As AI Boosts GDP; S&P 500 Rises (Live Coverage)
summary: The Federal Reserve's primary inflation measure hit its highest level since November 2023, while first-quarter GDP growth slightly undershot forecasts, despite a huge boost in spending on AI-related equipment and software.  The S&P 500 rose modestly after the data as investors weighed earnings reports and capital spending plans from Google, Amazon.com, Meta Platforms and Microsoft.  The inflation data follows Wednesday's hawkish policy signals at the final Fed meeting presided over by Chairman Jerome Powell.

headline: Oil prices whipsaw while US stocks glide near their record heights
summary: Oil prices whipsawed on Thursday and surged toward their highest levels since the war with Iran began, only for the leaps to quickly vanish.  The U.S. stock market, meanwhile, is gliding following more strong profit reports from big companies like Alphabet and Amazon.  The S&P 500 rose 0.2% and inched closer to its all-time high set earlier this week, as companies continue to deliver stronger profits for the start of 2026 than analysts expected despite high oil prices and uncertainty about the economy.

content.title: Oil prices whipsaw while US stocks glide near their record heights

The S&P 500 rose 0.2% and inched closer to its all-time high set earlier this week, as companies continue to deliver stronger profits for the start of 2026 than analysts expected despite high oil prices and uncertainty about the economy.
